sections.paragraph:
i.q. רָעַע-

(1) pr. to make a loud noise, see Hiphil.

(2) to be evil, see Niphal.

The forms of the conjugations of Kal (רַע, רֹעַ) and Hiphil (הֵרֵעַ, הֵרַע), which are commonly placed here, belong to the verb רָעַע, see Ewald’s Gram. p. 472.

Niphal, fut. יִרִֹועַ-

(1) to become evil, to be made worse (opp. to, to become wise), Pro 13:20.

(2) to suffer evil, to receive injury, Pro 11:15. (The noun רַע is added in this place intensitively, in the manner of an infinitive absolute.)

Hiphil הֵרִיעַ pl. also הֵרֵעוּ 1Sa 17:20, pr. to make a loud noise (lermen, Lerm machen); hence-

(1) to cry out with a loud voice, to vociferate, Job 30:5 specially-(a) of warlike clamour (תְּרוּעָה), Jos 6:16 1Sa 17:20.-(b) to shout for joy, Jud. 15:14 1Sa 10:24 followed by עַל over a conquered enemy, Psa 41:13 followed by a dat., in any one’s honour, Psa 47:2 95:2 .-(c) more rarely used of a mournful cry, Mic 4:9 Isa 15:4.

(2) to sound a trumpet. Num 10:9, הֲרֵעֹותֶם בַּחֲצֹצְרֹות “sound with the trumpets.” Joe 2:1 specially to sound an alarm, to sound the trumpets with a great noise (Lerm blafen), as a signal for the encampment to move, -Num 10:17, i.q. תָּקַע תְּרוּעָה Num 10:5, Num 10:6different from תָּקַע, which is to blow the trumpet (once) to call an assembly. Compare יֹובֵל No. 1.

Palel, fut. יְרֹעַע to be shouted for joy, Isa 16:10.

Hithpalel הִתְרֹועַע to shout for joy, Psa 60:10 65:14 108:10 .-The same form is found from the verb רָעַע, which see.

Derivatives, תְּרוּעָה [“and רֵעַ”].
